title: No `files` allowlist — publish will include entire project tree
reasoning: The package.json has no `files` field (and no `.npmignore` is evidenced). On publish, npm/bun will include every file in the project directory — source TypeScript under `engine/`, `ops/`, `bench/`, `cli/`, test files, config files, and potentially `.env` or other sensitive local files — in addition to the intended `dist/` and `scripts/`. This bloats the published tarball, increases install time, and risks leaking source code or secrets.
reproduction: Run `bun pm pack --dry-run` (or `npm pack --dry-run`) and observe that all project files are listed in the tarball, not just `dist/`, `scripts/`, and `package.json`.
recommendation: Add a `files` allowlist to package.json, e.g. `"files": ["dist", "scripts"]`, so only the runtime-necessary artifacts are published.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature. Tarball contents are a publish-time concern not covered by unit or integration tests.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI check: `bun pm pack --dry-run 2>&1 | grep -c 'engine/' | grep '^0$'` (or equivalent) to assert source directories are excluded from the tarball.
minimumFixScope: Add a `files` array to package.json listing only the directories needed at runtime (`dist`, `scripts`).
